reasoning: Given that the absorbance (A) is directly proportional to the molar absorptivity (ε), concentration (c), and path length (l), and inversely proportional to the transmittance (T), we can start by experimenting with the molar absorptivity.
Since the absorbance is directly proportional to the molar absorptivity, we will increase the molar absorptivity to observe its effect on the absorbance.
To further investigate the relationship between absorbance and concentration, we will decrease the molar absorptivity to observe its effect on absorbance when the concentration remains constant.

Since the absorbance is directly proportional to the concentration and path length, we will experiment with the concentration to observe its effect on absorbance when the molar absorptivity and path length remain constant.

The absorbance (A) is directly proportional to the molar absorptivity (ε), concentration (c), and path length (l), and inversely proportional to the transmittance (T). We have established that increasing the molar absorptivity, concentration, and path length increases the absorbance. However, we don't have enough data to determine the exact relationship between these variables. To get a clearer picture, we should also experiment with decreasing the variables and observe the changes in absorbance. After that, we can establish the final equation.
